vitals.surface: gravel, dirt tracks, doubletrack, some tarmac
vitals.aid_stations: 2 checkpoints (Berane, Peshkopi); remote with little resupply

course_description.overview: Fixed-route 1600 km off-road ultra starting and finishing in Shkoder, Albania, through the Accursed Mountains and Balkans, featuring relentless climbing over diverse terrain including gravel tracks, karst plateaus, and remote highlands.[1][2][3]

key_segments.name: Shkoder to Theth Valley
key_segments.description: From Lake Skadar wetlands to Dolomite-like crags in Theth valley, Albania.[2]

key_segments.name: Montenegro Karst to CP1 Berane
key_segments.description: Ciro Trail, old railway lines, above Mostar to Sutjeska National Park, Durmitor peaks, to Berane.[2]

key_segments.name: Katun Roads to CP2 Peshkopi
key_segments.description: Ancient transhumance roads to Kosovo border, tarmac respite, Lurë-Dejë mountain park with kettlehole lakes to Peshkopi.[2]

key_segments.name: Peshkopi to Shkoder Finish
key_segments.description: Relentless climbing and toughest doubletrack back to Shkoder.[2]

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Relentlessly hilly with 37,000m climbing across remote, challenging off-road terrain in Europe's last wild areas, including bear habitats and limited resupply.[2]

logistics.getting_there: No-fly policy; options include bus 'Accursed Express' from Zagreb to Shkoder (50 spots), trains/ferry from UK/Italy (e.g., Bari to Durres then coach), ~48h from UK.[2][4]
logistics.accommodation: Self-supported bikepacking; finish party in Shkoder.[2]
logistics.registration_details: Organized by Lost Dot; check lostdot.cc/tar for entry.[1][7]
logistics.bike_logistics: Off-road bikepacking setup recommended; no-fly requires land/sea transport.[3][4]
logistics.local_considerations: Remote Balkans terrain; potential bad weather, bears in Sutjeska; wild rivers and high altitudes under 2000m.[2][4]

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic inaugural bikepacking odyssey through Balkan wilds by Lost Dot.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Hardcore self-supported adventurers seeking remote off-road suffering in stunning mountains.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for those craving true exploration despite no-fly logistics.
pro_tips: Prepare for 37k+ m climbing and remoteness with ample supplies.
Opt for gravel bike with ample gear capacity.
Study no-fly routes like ferry from Bari.
